beats.activity: Wake up, check sourdough starter, make coffee and breakfast
beats.reasoning: Viktor's routine: first thing is checking the bulk fermentation from yesterday. Warm Friday means good rising conditions. Morning routine at home before heading to the jobsite.

beats.activity: Drive south to Tukwila jobsite via I-5
beats.reasoning: Established commute from U-District to Tukwila construction site. Early departure avoids peak traffic on a Friday morning.

beats.activity: On-site project management: framing inspection, subcontractor coordination, budget tracking
beats.reasoning: Full morning on the jobsite. Warm weather is ideal for exterior work; Viktor will be coordinating crews and managing the framing details that were problematic yesterday.

beats.activity: Lunch at jobsite or nearby: sandwich and hydration (77F high, warm day)
beats.reasoning: Viktor stays on-site for lunch to keep pace with the crew. Heat wave conditions demand hydration; he'll eat light and keep working.

beats.activity: Afternoon project management: material deliveries, crew scheduling, expense spreadsheet updates
beats.reasoning: Afternoon push on the jobsite. Friday wrap-up: finalizing the week's work, tracking expenses (Viktor's meticulous spreadsheet habit), and prepping for Monday's crew assignments.

beats.activity: Drive north from Tukwila to U-District, avoiding Capitol Hill Pride road closures (Broadway/Pike closed from 5pm)
beats.reasoning: Leaving site by 5pm avoids the worst of the Pride street fair traffic. Viktor stays clear of Capitol Hill—not his scene—and takes I-5 northbound back to U-District.

beats.activity: Quick errand: pick up fresh flour and salt at QFC for weekend sourdough baking
beats.reasoning: Friday evening supply run. Viktor stocks up for the weekend baking cycle; warm weather means faster fermentation, so fresh supplies are needed.

beats.activity: Return home, unpack groceries, feed sourdough starter, begin evening prep
beats.reasoning: Back at home base. Viktor checks on the bulk fermentation, refreshes the starter, and settles in for the evening. No plans to go out to Pride; he prefers quiet, focused time.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, eat, and spend time shaping dough and stretch-folding the bulk fermentation
beats.reasoning: Grounding evening routine: cooking dinner, then hands-on sourdough work. This is Viktor's preferred way to decompress after a long week managing the construction site.

beats.activity: Tidy kitchen, review Friday expenses in spreadsheet, light reading or prep for weekend
beats.reasoning: Wind-down at home: expense tracking (Viktor's habit), kitchen cleanup, and settling in for the night. No late outings; he's ready for rest.
Full night's sleep, ending well before dawn. Viktor maintains a disciplined sleep schedule to be fresh for early jobsite starts.

diary: Started the day like always—sourdough starter bubbling away, coffee steaming, the quiet of the U-District morning before the chaos. I-5 was its usual mess, but I made good time down to Tukwila. The framing inspection went smooth; the subcontractors are finally on schedule, which is a relief. Grabbed a sandwich at lunch and actually felt the heat—77 degrees, perfect for June. Drove back north around two, smart timing before the Pride closures hit Broadway and Pike. Grabbed flour and salt at QFC, thinking about the weekend batches I've got planned. Back home, fed the starter, got dinner going. Spent the evening folding dough, listening to the sounds of the city getting ready to celebrate. There's something peaceful about working with your hands after a day of spreadsheets and problem-solving.
thought_process: Today was about rhythm and balance. I kept an eye on the clock leaving Tukwila—Pride weekend means Seattle gets loud and celebratory, and I respect that, but I wanted to be home before the mess. Work was solid; the project's tracking well, which took pressure off mentally. I think the warm weather helped my mood; there's something about June that makes the sourdough baking feel less like a chore and more like a ritual. I chose to get groceries early rather than tomorrow because I knew the street fair would make everything chaotic. The evening routine with the dough felt grounding after managing budgets and crews all day. Even though I'm not heading downtown to Pride myself, I could feel the city's energy building, and there's something nice about being in your own space while the neighborhood celebrates around you.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
